data.title: Ethereum's In A 'Death Spiral,' Report Says—And Co-Founder Vitalik Buterin Seems To Agree
data.description: A recent report by Culper Research critiqued Ethereum (CRYPTO: ETH), citing concerns about its economic viability and suggesting the network might be headed towards a "death spiral." The report also targeted Tom Lee from Fundstrat, accusing him of continuing to invest in ETH through his company BitMine Immersion Technologies (NYSE: BMNR) despite potential issues. Titled "What Vitalik Knows, and Tom Lee Doesn't," the report highlighted the failed expectations of the Fusaka upgrade, indicating a significant drop in transaction fees rather than the expected reduction. This led to a substantial decrease in the EIP-1559 burn mechanism that previously helped make ETH deflationary. Culper Research suggested that Vitalik and validators underestimated Layer-1 demand elasticity by 3-9 times due to outdated calculations. Staking yields were reported to have fallen below U.S. Treasuries. The analysis covered all ETH transactions between January 2025 and February 2026. The full article is accessible on Benzinga.com for more detailed information.

data.title: Notification under Chapter 9, Section 10 of the Finnish Securities Market Act: voting rights of FMR LLC in Nokia Corporation exceeded 5%
data.description: On March 6, 2026, Nokia Corporation announced that under Chapter 9, Section 10 of the Finnish Securities Market Act, FMR LLC now holds voting rights exceeding 5% in Nokia Corporation. This notification was released at 22:00 EET.

data.title: Kraken introduces xChange for tokenized stock trading, offering 70+ equities on Ethereum and Solana.
data.description: The recently launched onchain trading engine on xStocks platform facilitates the exchange of over 70 tokenized equities on both the Ethereum and Solana networks.

data.title: Bitcoin Plunges 4% And Hitting $45,000 In 2026 Is A Coinflip's Chance, Polymarket Says
data.description: The price of Bitcoin (BTC) fell below $70,000 following the unexpected loss of 92,000 jobs in the U.S. economy for February. Macro analyst Benjamin Cowen warned that the rally in March may have been a bull trap which is now concluded. Nonfarm payrolls showed a significant decline of 92,000, well below the expected 59,000 and a stark contrast to January's 126,000 increase. Unemployment rate rose to 4.4%. The SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY) dropped below 6,800 points while oil was on track for its largest weekly increase since the early stages of the Ukraine conflict. Cowen pointed out that the Federal Reserve is in a challenging position, unable to reduce rates significantly to support the labor market due to soaring energy prices linked to the Iran tensions. Job openings per unemployed worker fell below 1.0. For more details, visit a financial news platform.

data.title: Kyivstar ranks among top global providers of Starlink Mobile satellite connectivity with 5 million total users
data.description: Kyivstar Group Ltd, the parent company of JSC Kyivstar, announced during Mobile World Congress 2026 that 5 million of its customers in Ukraine have now connected to the Kyivstar network through Starlink Mobile satellites. Kyivstar Group is part of VEON Group, and its stocks are listed on Nasdaq under the symbols KYIV and KYIVW, while VEON Group is listed under Nasdaq as VEON. This development signifies a significant milestone for Kyivstar in expanding its network reach and services to customers.

data.title: Shareholder Alert: The Ademi Firm investigates whether Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. is obtaining a Fair Price for its Public Shareholders
data.description: A law firm based in Milwaukee, Ademi LLP, has commenced an investigation into Day One (NASDAQ: DAWN) for potential breaches of fiduciary duty and other legal violations related to their recent deal with Servier. Individuals interested in participating in the investigation and receiving more information can find details by clicking the link provided.
